ip_outcome_note: Card name matches. PokemonTCG.io confirms swsh3-147 is Staraptor with National Pokedex no. 398. Art description — large bird with dark plumage, white facial marking, swept-back crest, red spot near eye — matches Staraptor's canonical design. Verification unambiguous.

trivia_bullets.text: Staraptor is National Pokedex no. 398, a Normal/Flying-type introduced in Generation IV (Diamond & Pearl, 2006). It is the final evolution of the Starly line: Starly → Staravia (level 14) → Staraptor (level 34). [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-147; WebSearch: Bulbapedia/Staraptor]

Flavor text on this card: 'When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.' The lore of solitary post-evolution departure is consistent across the Pokedex entries for Staraptor. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-147]

Staraptor has two abilities in the video games: Intimidate (lowers opposing Attack on entry) and the hidden ability Reckless (boosts recoil moves by 20%). Competitively, Smogon-tier analysis rates Reckless as the preferred ability in OU play, powering Brave Bird and Double-Edge at the cost of rapid self-damage — consistent with this card's Brave Bird attack (170 damage, 30 recoil to itself). Stealth Rock weakness and recoil accumulation are cited as Staraptor's primary longevity checks. [WebSearch: Smogon/Staraptor OU; PokemonDB]
